summary: Filled all user-facing documentation gaps for the new test module. Created docs/modules/testing.md as the canonical user reference (lifecycle, config, tags, isolation, staleness, timeouts, failure output, sensitive masking, report schemas, coverage tables, actions reference, known limitations). Updated docs/modules/index.md with the test row and appended the notContains action to the assert row. Added notContains to docs/modules/assert.md. Cross-referenced the new user doc from Documentation/v0.2/building_plang_tests.md. Appended a Test Module cross-cutting invariants section to Documentation/v0.2/good_to_know.md. Verified XML docs on the new C# handlers and Test.* types are already thorough — no code-level doc gaps to fill.

changes.description: New user-facing reference for the test module — lifecycle, CLI config, tags, isolation, staleness, timeouts, failure output + [Sensitive] masking, .test/ artefacts (json + junit), coverage tables, full actions reference, known security limitations from security-report.json findings 1-3.
Added the test module row to Events & Testing, and added notContains to the assert module's action list.
Documented the notContains action added on this branch (symmetric in argument order, same container types as contains).
Added a lead paragraph distinguishing authoring (this file) from runtime concerns (docs/modules/testing.md).

Appended 'Test Module — Cross-Cutting Invariants' covering nine non-obvious facts future devs won't see in any single file: App boundary = file boundary; Coverage.Merge is additive/idempotent; site key includes goal path; test.discover seeds declared chains so unreached sites still appear; [RequiresCapability] is class-level single-instance; staleness uses goal.Hash not mtime; ChildAppCreated is an internal test-only event; test.tag no-ops outside test mode; Variables.Snapshot honors exclusions not [Sensitive] (sensitive filter applies at serialization).

findings.issue: The test module had no user-facing documentation before this session. docs/modules/ covered assert and mock but not the runner users invoke with --test.
findings.detail: Created docs/modules/testing.md as a full reference.

findings.issue: The module index did not list the test module despite it being the most visible new user-facing surface on this branch. Also did not list notContains on the assert row.
findings.detail: Added test row and notContains action.

findings.issue: The notContains action exists in code (PLang/App/modules/assert/notContains.cs) but was not documented.
findings.detail: Added an action section mirroring the contains entry, plus a note on symmetric argument order.

findings.issue: The authoring reference was the only test-related doc but never pointed readers to how the runner actually executes, leaving runtime semantics undiscoverable.
findings.detail: Added a lead paragraph that distinguishes authoring (this file) from runtime (new testing.md).

findings.issue: Nine cross-cutting invariants of the test module are not stated anywhere — each is plausibly derivable from a single file, but the combined picture (App boundary = file boundary, coverage merge semantics, site-key format, chain seeding, RequiresCapability attribute constraints, staleness mechanism, ChildAppCreated as test-only hook, test.tag production no-op, Variables.Snapshot vs [Sensitive]) requires reading ~7 files to assemble.
findings.detail: Appended a 'Test Module — Cross-Cutting Invariants' section.

findings.issue: Authoring examples beyond the minimal one embedded in testing.md are the tester's job. Flagging for awareness; not a blocker since Tests/TestModule/**/*.test.goal already provides broad coverage.
findings.detail: The user doc includes one minimal .test.goal and one meta-test example. Additional patterns (fixture dirs, isolation probes, sensitive-variable handling) live in Tests/TestModule/ and don't need to be duplicated into docs/.
